title: Stelis suinii
intro: Stelis suinii is a species of orchid, specifically a type of epiphytic orchid that grows in tropical regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and it has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and fever, due to its potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Stelis suinii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
taxonomy: Stelis suinii is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This family comprises around 30,000 species of orchids, which is one of the largest plant families in the world. Stelis suinii, specifically, is part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae. This subtribe is a group of orchids that are primarily found in the Americas, with many species native to Central and South America. Within the subtribe, Stelis suinii is further classified into a specific genus and species classification.
